target.title: fix: cap undici keep-alive socket lifetime to prevent UND_ERR_SOCKET fallback
target.body:
Closes #87407

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where long-running gateways silently fall back from
Anthropic to OpenAI/Codex mid-conversation after 1-3 hours of uptime.
Undici's default `keepAliveMaxTimeout` of 600s (10 minutes) allows pooled
HTTP/1.1 connections to outlive the server-side idle timeout (30-120s for
Cloudflare/Anthropic/OpenAI). When undici attempts to reuse a connection
the server has already closed, it surfaces `UND_ERR_SOCKET` with no
transparent retry — triggering silent provider fallback.

Observed impact (#87407): 14 UND_ERR_SOCKET failures and 29 fallback
events over a ~4-hour window, clustered in bursts separated by 30-90
minute quiet periods.

## Why This Change Was Made

Sets `keepAliveMaxTimeout: 25_000` (25s) on all undici Agent constructors
created through `withHttp1OnlyDispatcherOptions`. The value sits below the
shortest known provider keep-alive window (~30s for Cloudflare) while still
allowing connection reuse across rapid sequential requests.

The cap respects explicit caller overrides — if a caller passes
`keepAliveMaxTimeout` in options, the default is not applied.

## User Impact

- Gateway connections no longer fail with UND_ERR_SOCKET after idle periods
- No configuration or config changes required
- Connection reuse still works for burst requests within the same turn

## Evidence

### Before: undici default keepAliveMaxTimeout

```
node_modules/undici/lib/dispatcher/client.js:267:
  this[kKeepAliveMaxTimeout] = keepAliveMaxTimeout == null ? 600e3 : keepAliveMaxTimeout

node_modules/undici/types/client.d.ts:54-55:
  /** maximum allowed idleTimeout when overridden by keep-alive hints.
   * Default: 600e3 milliseconds (10min). */
  keepAliveMaxTimeout?: number;
```

OpenClaw never set this value → undici defaulted to 600s (10min),
far exceeding the Cloudflare ~30s server-side close window.

### After: capped at 25s

```diff
+// Cap the maximum keep-alive socket lifetime below typical server-side idle
+// connection timeouts (30-120s for most cloud providers). Undici's default
+// keepAliveMaxTimeout is 600s (10 min), which lets connections outlive the
+// server's close window, causing UND_ERR_SOCKET and silent provider fallback.
+const KEEP_ALIVE_MAX_TIMEOUT_MS = 25_000;

+  // Bound the keep-alive socket lifetime to prevent undici from reusing
+  // connections that the server has already closed.
+  if (baseRecord.keepAliveMaxTimeout === undefined) {
+    baseRecord.keepAliveMaxTimeout = KEEP_ALIVE_MAX_TIMEOUT_MS;
+  }
```

### Runtime verification (real machine)

Intercepted undici Agent constructors at runtime to confirm
`keepAliveMaxTimeout` is injected:

```
undici 8.5.0 | Node v22.19.0

✅ Agent                keepAliveMaxTimeout=25000ms (before=600000ms → after=25000ms, -95.8%)
✅ ProxyAgent           keepAliveMaxTimeout=25000ms (before=600000ms → after=25000ms, -95.8%)
✅ EnvHttpProxyAgent    keepAliveMaxTimeout=25000ms (before=600000ms → after=25000ms, -95.8%)
```

All three Agent types (direct, explicit proxy, env proxy) now receive
`keepAliveMaxTimeout: 25000`, shutting down idle connections before
the server does.

### Test environment

| Item | Value |
|------|-------|
| OS | Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64 |
| Node.js | v22.19.0 |
| npm | 10.9.3 |
| pnpm | 11.2.2 |
| undici | 8.5.0 |
| Vitest | 4.1.8 |

### Test process

**Step 1: Modified module unit tests** (`pnpm test src/infra/net/undici-runtime.test.ts`)

```
 ✓ createHttp1ProxyAgent > adds active managed proxy CA trust
 ✓ createHttp1ProxyAgent > strips invalid IP SNI
 ✓ createHttp1ProxyAgent > strips invalid bracketed IPv6 SNI
 ✓ createHttp1ProxyAgent > preserves DNS SNI
 ✓ createHttp1EnvHttpProxyAgent > installs IP-safe proxy client factory
 ✓ keepAliveMaxTimeout > caps on direct undici Agent        ← new
 ✓ keepAliveMaxTimeout > caps on ProxyAgent                 ← new
 ✓ keepAliveMaxTimeout > caps on EnvHttpProxyAgent          ← new
 ✓ keepAliveMaxTimeout > preserves alongside caller timeout  ← new
 ✓ keepAliveMaxTimeout > respects explicit override          ← new

 Test Files  1 passed (1)  |  Tests  10 passed (10)
```

**Step 2: DNS/SSRF dispatcher tests** (`pnpm test src/infra/net/ssrf.dispatcher.test.ts`)

```
 ✓ 11 passed — all assertions verify keepAliveMaxTimeout: 25000 on Agent ctor
 Test Files  1 passed (1)  |  Tests  11 passed (11)
```

**Step 3: SSRF fetch guard tests** (`pnpm test src/infra/net/fetch-guard.ssrf.test.ts`)

```
 ✓ 85 passed — all assertions verify keepAliveMaxTimeout: 25000 on Agent ctor
 Test Files  1 passed (1)  |  Tests  85 passed (85)
```

**Step 4: Full net infra suite** (`pnpm test src/infra/net/`)

```
 [unit-fast]  Test Files  3 passed  |  Tests  116 passed
 [infra]      Test Files  11 passed |  Tests  257 passed

 Total: 14 Test Files, 373 Tests, all passed
```

### Test conclusions

- 5 new `keepAliveMaxTimeout` tests covering direct Agent, ProxyAgent,
  EnvHttpProxyAgent, timeout coexistence, and explicit caller override
- 368 existing tests all pass with zero regressions
- All Agent constructor assertions verify `keepAliveMaxTimeout: 25000`
  is correctly injected

### Files changed

```
 src/infra/net/undici-runtime.ts        | 17 ++++  (core logic)
 src/infra/net/undici-runtime.test.ts   | 74 ++++++  (5 new tests)
 src/infra/net/ssrf.dispatcher.test.ts  |  8 ++   (assertion alignment)
 src/infra/net/fetch-guard.ssrf.test.ts |  2 +    (assertion alignment)
 4 files changed, 99 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)
```

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
